english: Hind. a saw.
a mystical and symbolical word, Was. (183)
resp. bżes-rág (bzhes rag) C., don-rág (don rag) W., arrack, brandy, the usual barley-brandy, which is distilled in the convents and in nearly every manor-house.
1. prob. Ssk. a medicinal plant, Med. - 2. v. 'a. ('a)
myrobalan, an astringent medicinal fruit Med. frq.
a species of garlic, with a pale-red blossom, Allium strictum.
an interj. the meaning of which is stated differently, Mil.; 'a-re pạṅs ('a re phangs) well then! throw it away!
Sch.: = rogs-po (rogs po), grogs (grogs), companion, mate, fellow, comrade, friend; 'a-rog-kya ('a rog kya) or gya (gya) Sch.: 'a complimentary phrase or form of salutation'.

Mil., also 'ál-la ('al la) id. interj. expressive of joyful surprise: aye, ah, that is capital! dés-na 'á-la-la (des na 'a la la) well, that is excellent or splendid indeed! Mil.; also adjectively: *de saṅ di 'á-la-la* (de sang di 'a la la) W. this is much better than that!
the Tibetan vowels, 'ā-li-kā-li ('a li ka li) the series of the vowels together with the consonants; 'a-preṅ ('a phreng) id.
a little C.
a swallow Cs.
Sch. buckle, clasp (?).
a ring.
v. sub gtúm-mo (gtum mo).
apricot.
n. of a tree and of a king.
Sch.: a thick sauce or broth, soup; 'a-sbyár ('a sbyar) a thin broth.
for srú-mo (sru mo) aunt Sch.
adv. openly, manifestly, publicly, = mṅon-súm-du (mngon sum du).
interj. expressing joy, pleasure, satisfaction.
yea, nay even (?).
bad C.
Melia Azedarachta, the 'neem' of Anglo-Indians, an important remedy for cutaneous diseases.
Glr., 'og-tsóm ('og tshom) Sch., resp. żal-tsóm (zhal tshom) Pth., beard of the chin, chin-tuft.

1. sbst. = doms? (doms) 'aṅ-rta ('ang rta), 'aṅ-ras ('ang ras) loin-cloth C., W.; 'aṅ-túṅ ('ang thung) under-garment, χιτών, (hence also to be used for shirt and shift); 'aṅ-rág ('ang rag) trowsers, breeches Pth. — 2. interj.: well then! now then! well! in French: eh bien! at the beginning of a speech also 'aṅ-ge ('ang ge), 'aṅ-ke ('ang ke), 'aṅ-ki ('ang ki), Mil., without any regard to rank.
or 'aṅ-ki ('ang ki), Ssk. figure, number, cipher, also 'aṅ-graṅs ('ang grangs), 'aṅ-yig ('ang yig) Cs.; the last word, acc. to others, means secret characters, cryptography.
Ssk. finger Do.
acc. to Lew. English, Hd. angrezi.
Sch. flies, winged insects.
W. white chalk.
Med., Sch.: cervical vertebra.
C.: stocks, *'an-dó-la júg-pa* ('an do la 'jug pa) to put (a person) into the stocks.
C. 1. board, plank, deal. — 2. lcags-kyi 'an-°dár (lcags kyi 'an 'dar) Cs. an iron instrument of torture; acc. to Thgy. a kind of press.
Lex., corrupted 5 form for 'indra-nī-la ('indra ni la).
*'am-čog* ('am cog), *'am-bag* ('am bag) col. C. for rna-mčog (rna mchog), snam-brag (snam brag) ear; bosom.
a Chinese resident, Chinese superior civil officer, in the chief cities and provincial towns of the tributary countries of China.
